briefdescription.para: A vector mapping one coordinate to another.
Largest buffer size accepted for this buffer.
Smallest valid buffer size.
The current size of the buffer.
The orientation of the buffer layout.
The characters in the buffer.
A map, _rowRemap[addressed row] -> stored row
A map, _columnRemap[addressed column] -> stored column
Creates a 1x1 vertical buffer filled with a space.

detaileddescription.para: Usually only used as a placeholder until resized.

briefdescription.para: Construct a buffer with the given size and fill it with an initial block.

parameterdescription.para: The dimensions of the buffer. Size must be at least 1x1.
The orientation of the buffer. Cannot be changed after creation.
The optional fill character for the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Get the current size of the buffer.
Get the rectangle covering the whole buffer.

simplesect.para: A rectangle with origin (0,0) and the current size.

briefdescription.para: Read the block stored at the given logical position.

parameterdescription.para: The logical coordinates inside the buffer.

simplesect.para: A reference to the stored block, or a shared space block for invalid positions.

briefdescription.para: Create an independent writable copy of this buffer.

simplesect.para: A shared pointer to the cloned buffer.

briefdescription.para: Resize this buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the fast resize path. The internal mapping is rebuilt and the visible content order is undefined after the operation. Use resize(size, BufferResizeMode::PreserveContent, fillChar) if you need to preserve the visible order.
newSize
The new size of the buffer.
std::invalid_argument
if newSize is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (Size size, BufferResizeMode mode, Char fillChar) override
memberdef.name: resize

briefdescription.para: Resize this buffer and optionally keep the visible content order.

detaileddescription.para:
A preserve-content resize is fast when only the primary orientation axis changes. If the secondary axis changes, preserving content requires rebuilding the logical content and is expensive.
size
The new size.
mode
BufferResizeMode::PreserveContent keeps the visible order and fills new cells with fillChar. BufferResizeMode::Fast resizes using the fastest path and leaves the visible order undefined.
fillChar
The fill character for newly created cells in preserve-content mode. In fast mode it is only used to initialize newly appended storage cells.
std::invalid_argument
if size is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Write a block at the given logical position.

detaileddescription.para:
This mirrors the wide-character handling from Buffer: zero-width blocks are ignored, width-2 blocks occupy the next logical cell as an empty continuation cell, and width-2 blocks at the right edge are ignored.
pos
The logical coordinates within the buffer.
block
The block to write.

briefdescription.para: Reserve memory for the given buffer size.

parameterdescription.para: The size whose capacity should be reserved.

briefdescription.para: Shift the buffer in the given direction, fill new cells with a given character.

parameterdescription.para: The character to fill new cells with.
The number of cells to shift.
if count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Rotate the buffer in the given direction.

detaileddescription.para:
Cells are shifted in a circular manner, wrapping around to the other end of the buffer.
direction
The direction in which to rotate.
count
The number of cells to rotate.
std::invalid_argument
if count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: Erase rows in the buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
This will erase count rows, starting from startRow, and insert empty ones at the end. If you like to actually shrink the buffer, use resize after this call.
startRow
The first row to delete.
fillChar
The character to fill new cells with.
count
The number of rows to delete.
std::invalid_argument
if startRow is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Erase columns in the buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
This will erase count columns, starting from startColumn, and insert empty ones at the end. If you like to actually shrink the buffer, use resize after this call.
startColumn
The first column to delete.
fillChar
The character to fill new cells with.
count
The number of columns to delete.
std::invalid_argument
if startColumn is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Insert rows in the buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
The rows at the bottom of the buffer will be erased to make room for the new rows. If you like to actually grow the buffer, use resize before this call.
startRow
The first row to insert.
fillChar
The character to fill the new rows with.
count
The number of rows to insert.
std::invalid_argument
if startRow is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Insert columns in the buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
The columns on the right side of the buffer will be erased to make room for the new columns. If you like to actually grow the buffer, use resize before this call.
startColumn
The first column to insert.
fillChar
The character to fill the new columns with.
count
The number of columns to insert.
std::invalid_argument
if startColumn is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (Coordinate startRow, int count, Coordinate delta, Char fillChar)
memberdef.name: moveRows

briefdescription.para: Move rows in the buffer by a given delta.

detaileddescription.para:
A positive delta moves rows down, a negative delta moves rows up. This reshuffles the moved rows, but rows that get moved out of the buffer area are deleted and replaced by empty cells using the fillChar.
startRow
The first row to move.
count
The number of rows to move.
delta
The number of positions to move (positive = down, negative = up).
fillChar
The character to fill vacated cells with.
std::invalid_argument
if startRow is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Coordinate startRow, const int count, const Coordinate delta)
memberdef.name: moveRows

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (Coordinate startColumn, int count, Coordinate delta, Char fillChar)
memberdef.name: moveColumns

briefdescription.para: Move columns in the buffer by a given delta.

detaileddescription.para:
A positive delta moves columns right, a negative delta moves columns left. This reshuffles the moved columns, but columns that get moved out of the buffer area are deleted and replaced by empty cells using the fillChar.
startColumn
The first column to move.
count
The number of columns to move.
delta
The number of positions to move (positive = right, negative = left).
fillChar
The character to fill vacated cells with.
std::invalid_argument
if startColumn is out of bounds or count is negative or exceeds buffer size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Coordinate startColumn, const int count, const Coordinate delta)
memberdef.name: moveColumns

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Fill/clear the buffer with the given character.

simplesect.para: This will also reset the internal remapping indexes.

parameterdescription.para: The block to use to fill the buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Bitmap &bitmap, Position pos, const BitmapDrawOptions &options=BitmapDrawOptions::defaultOptions(), std::size_t animationCycle=0) noexcept
memberdef.name: drawBitmap

briefdescription.para: Draw a bitmap at a given position.

detaileddescription.para:
The bitmap is rendered according to options.scaleMode(). If options.char16Style() is set, it overrides the scale mode and renders one terminal cell per bitmap pixel. Pixels or rendered cells outside the buffer are ignored.
bitmap
The bitmap to draw.
pos
The position of the top left corner.
options
Bitmap drawing options.
animationCycle
Animation cycle for color animations.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Bitmap &bitmap, Rectangle rect, Alignment alignment=Alignment::TopLeft, const BitmapDrawOptions &options=BitmapDrawOptions::defaultOptions(), std::size_t animationCycle=0) noexcept
memberdef.name: drawBitmap

briefdescription.para: Draw a bitmap into the given rectangle.

detaileddescription.para:
The rendered bitmap is aligned inside rect. If it is larger than rect, it is cropped according to the alignment.
For half-block drawing mode, alignment and cropping happen at rendered cell boundaries, not per pixel.
bitmap
The bitmap to draw.
rect
The rectangle to draw the bitmap into.
alignment
Alignment of the bitmap within the rectangle.
options
Bitmap drawing options.
animationCycle
Animation cycle for color animations.

briefdescription.para: Draw a text without warping from the given position.

detaileddescription.para:
A newline breaks to the next line, starting at pos.x. Characters outside this buffer are cut off.
pos
The start position (top-left corner).
str
The text to draw on this buffer.

briefdescription.para: If fg or bg is set to Inherited, the current color from the buffer is used.

detaileddescription.para:
Draw simple text into a rectangle. If fg or bg is set to Inherited, the current color from the buffer is used.
text
The text description.
animationCycle
Animation cycle for animated text.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::string_view text, Rectangle rect, Alignment alignment=Alignment::TopLeft, CharStyle style={}, std::size_t animationCycle=0)
memberdef.name: drawText

briefdescription.para: Draw simple text into a rectangle.

detaileddescription.para:
If fg or bg is set to Inherited, the current color from the buffer is used.
text
The text to render.
rect
The target rectangle.
alignment
The alignment inside the rectangle.
style
The base text style.
animationCycle
Animation cycle for animated text. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::u32string_view text, Rectangle rect, Alignment alignment=Alignment::TopLeft, CharStyle style={}, std::size_t animationCycle=0)
memberdef.name: drawText

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (const StringView &text, Rectangle rect, Alignment alignment=Alignment::TopLeft, CharStyle style={}, std::size_t animationCycle=0)
memberdef.name: drawText

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (const StringView &text, Rectangle rect, const TextOptions &options, std::size_t animationCycle=0)
memberdef.name: drawText

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Fill/clear the buffer with the given character.

parameterdescription.para: The block to use to fill the buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (Rectangle rect, const Char &fillBlock, const CharCombinationStylePtr &combinationStyle={}) noexcept
memberdef.name: fill

briefdescription.para: Fill the given rectangle.

detaileddescription.para:
Positions outside the buffer are ignored.
rect
The rectangle to be filled.
fillBlock
The block for filling.
combinationStyle
The combination style for overwriting existing characters.

memberdef.argsstring: (Rectangle rect, const Tile9StylePtr &style, Color baseColor={}, const CharCombinationStylePtr &combinationStyle={}) noexcept
memberdef.name: fill

briefdescription.para: Fill the given rectangle using a repeating 9-tile style.

detaileddescription.para:
Positions outside the buffer are ignored.
rect
The rectangle to be filled.
style
The tile style to repeat across the rectangle.
baseColor
The base color underneath the tile style.
combinationStyle
The combination style for overwriting existing characters.

memberdef.argsstring: (Rectangle rect, const Tile9StylePtr &style, CharStyle baseStyle, const CharCombinationStylePtr &combinationStyle={}) noexcept
memberdef.name: fill

briefdescription.para: Fill the given rectangle using a repeating 9-tile style.

detaileddescription.para:
Positions outside the buffer are ignored.
rect
The rectangle to be filled.
style
The tile style to repeat across the rectangle.
baseStyle
The base style underneath the tile style.
combinationStyle
The combination style for overwriting existing characters.

briefdescription.para: Resize this buffer in a memory-efficient way.

detaileddescription.para:
The content of the resized buffer is undefined and must be filled with new content.
newSize
The new size for the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Resize this buffer and optionally preserve visible content.

detaileddescription.para:
The default implementation calls resize(Size) for BufferResizeMode::Fast. For BufferResizeMode::PreserveContent, it clones the current buffer, resizes it using resize(Size), and restores the visible content with setFrom(). Implementations can override this when they provide a faster preserve-content path.
size
The new size for the buffer.
mode
How existing content should be handled during resizing.
fillChar
The character to fill newly visible cells with in preserve-content mode.

briefdescription.para: Write a block at the given position.

simplesect.para: Writes outside the buffer are ignored.

memberdef.argsstring: (Position pos, const Char &block, const CharCombinationStylePtr &combinationStyle) noexcept
memberdef.name: set

briefdescription.para: Write a block at the given position using a combination style.

parameterdescription.para: The combination style for overwriting existing characters.

simplesect.para: Writes outside the buffer are ignored.

briefdescription.para: Write a string at the given position.

detaileddescription.para:
NL jumps to the next row. Other control and zero-width characters are ignored. Color (even inherited) overwrites the existing characters. Use drawText(pos, text) for a color overlay.
pos
The coordinates within the buffer.
str
The string to write.

briefdescription.para: Validate the buffer size.
Create a linear index map for the given size.
Validate a count-only argument.

parameterdescription.para: The parameter name for error reporting.

memberdef.argsstring: (Coordinate start, int count, int limit, std::string_view startName, std::string_view countName)
memberdef.name: validateExistingSpan

briefdescription.para: Validate a span inside the currently visible range.

parameterdescription.para: The first coordinate in the span.
The number of elements in the span.
The size of the addressable axis.
The parameter name for the start coordinate.
The parameter name for the count.

memberdef.argsstring: (Coordinate start, int count, int limit, std::string_view startName, std::string_view countName)
memberdef.name: validateInsertArguments

briefdescription.para: Validate an insert-style operation on an axis.

parameterdescription.para: The size of the addressable axis.
The parameter name for the start coordinate.
The parameter name for the count.

memberdef.argsstring: (Position pos, Size size, Orientation orientation) noexcept
memberdef.name: bufferIndex

briefdescription.para: Get the buffer index for the given orientation and size.

parameterdescription.para: The size used to linearize the position.

briefdescription.para: Rotate a coordinate map to the logical front or back.

parameterdescription.para: The number of elements to rotate.
If true, rotate toward index 0, otherwise toward the logical end.

briefdescription.para: Erase a span from a coordinate map and append the recycled coordinates at the end.

parameterdescription.para: The number of elements to erase.

simplesect.para: The recycled coordinates that must be refilled.

briefdescription.para: Insert a span into a coordinate map using recycled coordinates from the end.

parameterdescription.para: The number of elements to insert.

simplesect.para: The recycled coordinates that must be refilled.

memberdef.argsstring: (CoordinateMap &map, Coordinate start, int count, Coordinate delta)
memberdef.name: moveInMap

briefdescription.para: Move a span inside a coordinate map.

parameterdescription.para: The map to transform in place.
The number of elements to move.

simplesect.para: The recycled coordinates that must be refilled.

briefdescription.para: Validate a directional count for shift and rotate.

parameterdescription.para: The number of cells to move.
if the count does not fit the addressed axis.

briefdescription.para: Remap the position.
Calculate the storage index for a stored coordinate pair.
Calculate the storage index for a logical coordinate pair.

simplesect.para: The linear storage index after remapping.

briefdescription.para: Get the storage index for the current size and orientation.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::span< const Coordinate > rows, const Char &fillChar) noexcept
memberdef.name: fillStoredRows

briefdescription.para: Fill the given stored rows.

parameterdescription.para: The stored row coordinates to fill.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::span< const Coordinate > columns, const Char &fillChar) noexcept
memberdef.name: fillStoredColumns

briefdescription.para: Fill the given stored columns.

parameterdescription.para: The stored column coordinates to fill.

briefdescription.para: Execute the fast resize path.

parameterdescription.para: The fill character for newly appended storage cells.

briefdescription.para: Execute the fast preserve-content path for primary-axis-only resizes.

parameterdescription.para: The fill character for newly created logical cells.

briefdescription.para: Execute the ordered resize path.

parameterdescription.para: The fill character for newly created logical cells.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the resize changes only the primary axis of the buffer orientation.

simplesect.para: true if only the orientation axis changes.

briefdescription.para: Access the remap for the orientation axis.

simplesect.para: The row map for vertical buffers or the column map for horizontal buffers.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Copy one stored primary line to another stored primary line.
A buffer that allows fast remapping/shifting/inserting/deleting of rows and columns.

detaileddescription.para: This buffer is useful if you have a large buffer that requires frequent row- or column-based manipulations. It is designed for fast insert/delete/move and shift operations.
This buffer also knows two orientations: vertical and horizontal.

listitem.para: In the vertical orientation, the buffer can efficiently grow vertically, keeping the existing data intact.
In the horizontal orientation, the buffer can efficiently grow horizontally, keeping the existing data intact.
When growing the buffer, memory reallocation may be necessary. Use reserve to reserve enough memory to avoid frequent reallocations.
Use resize(size, BufferResizeMode::PreserveContent, fillChar) when you need to preserve the visible content.
A preserve-content resize that changes only the primary orientation axis is optimized and fast.
A preserve-content resize that changes the secondary axis must rebuild the logical content and can be expensive.
